Learning Outcome: Classify information into the four levels (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted) using the definitions and examples; apply the correct minimum handling for each level
Apply electronic labels (document banner, filename tag, or Workspace label) to all Confidential/Restricted items; assign a data owner; mark physical media accordingly
Collect, keep, and copy only what is necessary; avoid local copies when cloud sharing suffices
Identify when CUI is present and treat it as Confidential with additional CUI-specific safeguards per the CUI Policy

Store Public/Internal data in approved locations (Google Workspace, approved repos/SaaS); store Confidential/Restricted only in access-controlled areas with audit logging; store CUI only in authorized CUI boundaries
Verify that all data in transit uses TLS 1.2+ with strong cipher suites; confirm that cloud/SaaS providers enforce TLS for all CivicActions data flows
Verify that cloud/SaaS providers enable encryption at rest for CivicActions data; enable full-disk encryption on all managed endpoints (FileVault/LUKS)

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Use only FIPS 140-2/140-3 validated modules for CUI at rest and in transit; validate FedRAMP equivalency for cloud services before CUI use

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Use cloud KMS/HSM or platform-managed keys with restricted roles, rotation, and logging; understand that key access is Restricted

Use managed endpoints for Confidential/Restricted access; keep data in the cloud; if temporary local storage is unavoidable, keep it on encrypted volumes and purge promptly; use secure connections and zero-trust posture for travel/home networks

Do not export PII or sensitive data from SaaS platforms for testing; store no secrets in source code; use SCA/SBOM and remediate vulnerabilities before release; include data handling checks as CI/CD gates

Share via Google Drive to named accounts (no "anyone with link") for Internal/Confidential/Restricted; do not use email for Confidential/Restricted; if a partner cannot access Drive, send as a password-protected AES-256 archive with a 20+ character passphrase transmitted via a separate channel
Follow the CUI Policy for CUI sharing; do not use Slack for CUI data; use only approved CUI boundaries and channels

Understand that Google Workspace DLP rules protect labels/keywords and block risky sharing; cooperate with endpoint controls and CIS baselines; know that DLP events and access anomalies are triaged and may escalate to IR

Avoid removable media and printing by default; if business-necessary and approved, encrypt media, scan for malware, maintain chain-of-custody, and record in the asset register; CUI on removable media is exception-only
Sync project work to approved cloud (Google Drive/repos); do not backup credentials to cloud; understand that System Owners test restores for critical datasets annually
Follow Data Owner–defined retention schedules; minimize retention by default; understand that legal holds override normal deletion

Section: Open Data and Public Release Controls
Learning Outcome: Before any public release, confirm Public classification, pass automated secrets/PII scans, obtain owner approval, review license, and confirm contract permits release if derived from client data

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Understand the remote-first sanitization methods: cryptographic erasure via MDM, validated wipe for returned devices, approved wiping tools for external media, and mail-in shredding for failed media; know that full-disk encryption is mandatory so key destruction renders data irrecoverable

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Ensure vendors touching CivicActions or client data meet baseline safeguards by contract with annual reviews; for CUI, require FedRAMP Moderate equivalency
Report immediately any suspected data incident (loss, exposure, mis-send, DLP block); know that incidents are handled under the IR Plan and post-incident learning updates controls

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Module: D - Endpoint posture and privileged access
Learning Outcome: Understand that evidence is maintained (classification attestations, DLP configs, encryption posture, backup tests, sanitization certificates, vendor reviews); track DLP incidents, restore success, time-to-revoke, and % managed endpoints
Understand that violations can result in access revocation, disciplinary action, or contract remedies
